title: Java Source
documentation:
## Purpose
Java source files contain code written in the Java programming language, a class-based, object-oriented language designed for platform independence via the Java Virtual Machine (JVM). Java is widely used in enterprise backends, Android development, and large-scale distributed systems.

## When to use
Use [Java Source](https://capdag.com/browse/media:java;textable;code) for `.java` files containing class, interface, enum, or annotation declarations. Java source is compiled to `.class` bytecode files before execution; this media type covers the source form only.

## Notes
Java source files are conventionally named after the public class they declare (one public class per file). The Java version (language level) affects available syntax features — records, sealed classes, and pattern matching were introduced in later versions. Build tools such as Maven and Gradle manage compilation and dependency resolution.
